mnemonics: Fork Like a Child, Use vfork
Wait for the Child, Use wait or waitpid
keyPoints: A process is identified by its process ID (PID)
fork() creates a new process and returns the PID of the child process
vfork() is a variation of fork() that allows the parent process to wait for the child before continuing execution
exit() allows a process to terminate its execution and return control to the parent process
wait() and waitpid() are used to wait for a child process to terminate before continuing execution in the parent process
wait3() is similar to waitpid(), but it also receives additional information about the child process that terminated
